artifact.title: COLD EMAIL
artifact.content:
I hope this message finds you well. I’m a Computer Science student at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign with experience building scalable systems and data-driven applications, and I’m very interested in exploring software engineering internship opportunities on your team.

I’ve worked on projects involving distributed systems, backend infrastructure, and AI-powered tools, and I’m particularly drawn to teams operating at large scale with strong engineering rigor. I would greatly value the opportunity to learn more about your work and any potential internship openings.

I’ve attached my resume for context. If you’re open to it, I would appreciate a brief conversation to better understand your team and how I might contribute.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Best,
Dhruv Malpani

payload.text: Dhruv's cold email is pretty straightforward and well-structured. He mentions experience in distributed systems and backend infrastructure, which aligns well with what we focus on in search optimization. I appreciate the clarity and relevance, but it could use a touch more personalization—mentioning a specific aspect of our team's work, like latency improvements or reliability [source: COLD EMAIL].

Maybe if Dhruv had mentioned something unique to the team or a recent project, it would show he's done his homework. Also, highlighting any specific technologies he's worked with that match our stack could be a good move. [uncertain: no explicit artifact citation in response]
